intro.speaker: Varn
intro.text: You returned. I was not certain you would. The dungeon discourages repetition — yet here you are again.

responses.text: Not yet. I need to go deeper first.
I remember you. What do you need?
How have you been holding up?

how_holding_up.speaker: Varn
how_holding_up.text: The chains fray a little more each day. But I endure. I have always endured. It is the only thing I know how to do well.

responses.text: What do you need from me this time?

the_ask.speaker: Varn
the_ask.text: There is something I require. It was taken from me long before the chains began to fray. A pair of grips — iron and chain, built for climbing, for pulling, for holding things in place. I believe you may have found them.

responses.text: The Grips of the Buried Flame? I have them.
I haven't come across anything like that.
Why should I give them to you?

why_give.speaker: Varn
why_give.text: Because they are mine. And because I can show you what they once allowed me to do.

the_flash.speaker: Varn
the_flash.text: For just a moment, the chains around him pull taut — then release. The air tastes of iron. He exhales slowly, composing himself. 'That. That is what I am suppressing. It is not gone. It is waiting.'

responses.text: I don't have them yet. I'll find them.

has_grips.speaker: Varn
has_grips.text: Then you know what they are capable of. I would ask you to return them to me. In exchange, I can offer you something that may serve you better on the path ahead.

give_grips.speaker: Varn
give_grips.text: Good. The chains remember this. You will find these carriers move differently than the grips — faster, lighter. Less suited for holding things in place. More suited for surviving.

refuse_grips.speaker: Varn
refuse_grips.text: Then keep them. I will not force the matter. But remember — the chains I hold are for everyone's benefit. Including yours. Think on that.

no_grips.speaker: Varn
no_grips.text: Then keep looking. They will be somewhere in these halls — the dungeon hoards what it takes. When you find them, bring them to me. I will make it worth your while.

find_them.speaker: Varn
find_them.text: I know you will. The dungeon does not give up what it has taken easily, but it gives to those who persist. Find them. Bring them back.
